items.title: The M5 MacBook Air Has Never Been Cheaper
items.summary: Despite launching less than two months ago, the M5 MacBook Air has dropped back down to its lowest price since launching.

items.title: Elon Musk Boosts New Yorker’s Sam Altman Exposé on X as Trial Begins
items.summary: The move comes as the trial for Elon Musk’s lawsuit against OpenAI kicks off in federal court in Oakland.

items.title: Cole Allen Charged With Attempting to Assassinate Trump
items.summary: The suspected shooter at Saturday night’s White House Correspondents’ Dinner faces three felony charges. He remains in custody following Monday’s hearing.

items.title: Letterboxd, the social platform for film buffs, reportedly looking for new owner
items.summary: Potential buyers of Letterboxd include Versant, the parent company of CNBC and MS NOW, and Hollywood media company The Ankler, according to Semafor.

items.title: Consumers lost $2.1B to social media scams in 2025, FTC reports
items.summary: The agency reports that losses from social media scams have increased eightfold and that social media scams resulted in higher losses than any other method scammers used to contact consumers.

items.title: OpenAI ends Microsoft legal peril over its $50B Amazon deal
items.summary: OpenAI has won major concessions from its largest shareholder, Microsoft, that will allow it to sell products on AWS, while Microsoft gets more cash in a revenue-share agreement.

items.title: Melania Trump urges ABC to 'take stand' on Jimmy Kimmel after widow joke
items.summary: In a parody aired days before the White House Correspondents' Dinner, Kimmel called Melania an "expectant widow".

items.title: Trump says King will be 'very safe' during US visit after security talks
items.summary: The state visit will go ahead despite concerns raised after a gunman targeted an event attended by the president.

items.title: Pakistan accused of attacking Afghan university
items.summary: Air attacks on the province of Kunar have killed at least seven people and injured 75, sources tell the BBC.

items.title: Ray Dalio says Kevin Warsh shouldn't cut interest rates in a ‘stagflation’ era
items.summary: Dalio said that if Kevin Warsh were to cut rates, it would risk damaging confidence in the central bank at a critical moment.

items.title: This bank CEO let his AI clone handle an earnings call — now he's signing an OpenAI deal
items.summary: It's part of Customers Bank CEO Sam Sidhu's effort to get ahead in the industry's race to transform itself using AI agents as a new digital workforce

items.title: This little-known ETF is up over 600% amid U.S.-Iran war, a better trade than oil or energy stocks
items.summary: The Breakwave Tanker Shipping ETF (BWET) has surged more than 600% since the beginning of the year, vastly outperforming crude oil and energy stocks.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.summary: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: Georgia governor candidates clash in debates as early voting starts
items.summary: Candidates for governor in Georgia faced off Monday in a pair of party debates as early voting kicked off in the Peach State. On the Republican stage at the Atlanta Press Club, billionaire Rick Jackson was put on defense as the crowded field of rivals traded jabs. In the Democrats’ showdown, candidates pitched plans to…

items.title: Johnson says he has ‘modified’ version of Senate DHS bipartisan bill
items.summary: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) said Monday that a bipartisan Senate bill to fund most of the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) includes “problematic” language and added that he has a “modified” version of the measure. The Senate bill, which was passed earlier this month, is part of a two-step process aimed at ending the record-breaking…

items.title: AT&T CEO hopes new offers will restore customer loyalty
items.summary: AT&T CEO hopes new offers will restore customer loyalty

items.title: Public Service Enterprise Group (PEG) Declares Dividend of $0.67 per Share
items.summary: Public Service Enterprise Group (PEG) Declares Dividend of $0.67 per Share

items.title: NRC Renews License for Duke Energy’s Robinson Nuclear Plant
items.summary: NRC Renews License for Duke Energy’s Robinson Nuclear Plant

items.title: This hidden kind of stress may be damaging your memory as you age
items.summary: A new study reveals that internalizing stress—especially feelings of hopelessness—may significantly speed up memory decline in older Chinese Americans. Surprisingly, factors like community support didn’t show the same impact. Researchers say cultural pressures and stereotypes may cause emotional struggles to go unnoticed and untreated. The findings suggest that targeted, culturally sensitive stress relief could play a powerful role in preserving cognitive health.

items.title: Students build a “cosmic radio” to listen for dark matter
items.summary: A group of undergraduate students pulled off something remarkable: they built their own dark matter detector and used it to probe one of physics’ biggest mysteries. Working with limited resources but plenty of creativity, they designed a stripped-down experiment to hunt for axions — hypothetical particles that could make up dark matter.

items.title: Scientists finally solve mystery of strange “golden orb” found 2 miles deep
items.summary: A mysterious “golden orb” found more than two miles deep in the Gulf of Alaska left scientists baffled for over two years, sparking wild speculation about its origins. After an intensive investigation combining deep-sea expertise, microscopic analysis, and advanced DNA sequencing, researchers finally cracked the case. The strange object turned out not to be an egg, sponge, or anything alien, but the remains of tissue from a giant deep-sea anemone.

items.title: This dangerous pregnancy complication is common. A new treatment might help
items.summary: Preeclampsia complicates 3 to 8 percent of pregnancies. In a recent trial, a blood filter lowered blood pressure and helped prolong some pregnancies.

items.title: The earliest evidence of the first stars may lie in a distant gas clump
items.summary: James Webb data reveal pristine gas irradiated by energetic light some 450 million years after the Big Bang — a sign it may house primordial stars.

items.title: Ancient DNA tests the notion that allergies are due to our dirtier past
items.summary: An analysis of ancient DNA and modern disease risk suggests some immune genes may reduce allergy risk rather than increase it.
